Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: The problem requires reconstructing a sequence of sibling-merge operations. This involves analyzing tree structures, identifying which nodes were merged, and determining the order of operations based on the parent-child relationships in the final tree compared to the initial tree.

helpers.label: tree_structure_analyzer
helpers.instructions: Given the initial tree and the final tree, identify the set of nodes that were merged into single nodes. Specifically, for each node in the final tree, identify which set of nodes from the initial tree it represents. Output a mapping of final-tree-nodes to their constituent initial-tree-nodes.

helpers.label: merge_order_validator
helpers.instructions: Using the mapping of final-tree-nodes to initial-tree-nodes, determine the valid sequence of sibling-merge operations. A merge operation takes two adjacent siblings and combines them. For each node in the final tree that represents multiple initial nodes, calculate the sequence of merges required to combine its initial-tree-node children into the final configuration, ensuring the order respects the sibling adjacency in the initial tree.

authored_response.synthesis_brief: Combine the mapping of final nodes to initial nodes with the calculated merge sequences to produce the complete, ordered list of sibling-merge operations that transforms the initial tree into the final tree.
